Exam.title: Exam
Exam.description: Represents an online examination configured by an admin, including its duration, availability, and pricing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Exam entity.

name.description: The name or title of the exam.

activeStartDate.description: The date and time when the exam becomes available for students.

activeEndDate.description: The date and time when the exam is no longer available.

durationMinutes.description: The maximum duration for the exam once a student starts it, in minutes.

price.description: The price of the exam in the local currency (e.g., Manat). A value of 0 indicates a free exam.

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a single question within an exam, defining its type, text, and order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

examId.description: Reference to the Exam this question belongs to. (Relationship: Exam 1:N Question)

text.description: The main text of the question.

imageUrl.description: Optional URL to an image associated with the question.

order.description: The display order of the question within the exam.

MultipleChoiceOption.title: MultipleChoiceOption
MultipleChoiceOption.description: Represents a single answer option for a multiple-choice question, indicating if it's the correct answer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MultipleChoiceOption entity.

questionId.description: Reference to the Question this option belongs to. (Relationship: Question 1:N MultipleChoiceOption)

text.description: The text content of the answer option.

isCorrect.description: Indicates if this option is the correct answer for the multiple-choice question.

OpenEndedQuestionCorrectAnswer.title: OpenEndedQuestionCorrectAnswer
OpenEndedQuestionCorrectAnswer.description: Stores the predefined correct answer for an open-ended question.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OpenEndedQuestionCorrectAnswer entity.

questionId.description: Reference to the Open-Ended Question this correct answer is for. (Relationship: Question 1:1 OpenEndedQuestionCorrectAnswer)

correctAnswerText.description: The exact text of the correct answer for the open-ended question.

ExplanationQuestionCriteria.title: ExplanationQuestionCriteria
ExplanationQuestionCriteria.description: Stores the correct final answer and detailed explanation criteria for an explanation-based question evaluated by AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExplanationQuestionCriteria entity.

questionId.description: Reference to the Explanation Question this criteria is for. (Relationship: Question 1:1 ExplanationQuestionCriteria)

correctFinalAnswer.description: The exact correct final numerical or textual answer expected from the student.

explanationCriteria.description: Detailed criteria or a model explanation used by the AI to evaluate the student's explanation.

ExamAccessCode.title: ExamAccessCode
ExamAccessCode.description: A unique, 6-character alphanumeric code generated for an exam, allowing a student one-time entry and subsequent results viewing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExamAccessCode entity.

code.description: The unique 6-character alphanumeric code for exam access.

examId.description: Reference to the Exam this code grants access to. (Relationship: Exam 1:N ExamAccessCode)

isUsedForEntry.description: Indicates if this code has already been used to start an exam.

studentAttemptId.description: Reference to the StudentAttempt created when this code was first used for entry. Null if not yet used. (Relationship: ExamAccessCode 1:1 StudentAttempt)

StudentAttempt.title: StudentAttempt
StudentAttempt.description: Represents a single student's attempt at taking an exam, linked to an access code and recording student details and overall progress.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentAttempt entity.

examId.description: Reference to the Exam being attempted. (Relationship: Exam 1:N StudentAttempt)

examAccessCodeId.description: Reference to the ExamAccessCode used for this attempt. (Relationship: ExamAccessCode 1:1 StudentAttempt)

studentFirstName.description: The first name of the student taking the exam.

studentLastName.description: The last name of the student taking the exam.

startTime.description: The date and time when the student started the exam.

endTime.description: The date and time when the student completed or submitted the exam. Null if not yet completed.

totalScore.description: The overall score obtained by the student for this attempt. Null until the exam is fully graded.

isCompleted.description: Indicates if the exam attempt has been completed and submitted.

StudentAnswer.title: StudentAnswer
StudentAnswer.description: Records a student's submitted answer and the corresponding evaluation results for a specific question within an exam attempt.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentAnswer entity.

studentAttemptId.description: Reference to the StudentAttempt this answer belongs to. (Relationship: StudentAttempt 1:N StudentAnswer)

questionId.description: Reference to the Question being answered. (Relationship: Question 1:N StudentAnswer)

submittedMultipleChoiceOptionId.description: The ID of the chosen option for multiple-choice questions.

submittedOpenEndedAnswerText.description: The student's submitted answer for open-ended questions.

submittedExplanationText.description: The student's submitted explanation for explanation questions.

submittedFinalAnswerText.description: The student's submitted final answer for explanation questions.

score.description: The score awarded for this specific answer (e.g., 0, 1/3, 1/2, 2/3, 1).

isCorrectFinalAnswer.description: Indicates if the student's final answer (for explanation questions), selected option (for MCQs), or submitted text (for open-ended) matches the correct one.

aiExplanationFeedback.description: AI-generated feedback on the student's explanation for explanation questions.

feedbackReason.description: Details on why a specific score was awarded or deducted for explanation questions, based on AI analysis.

answeredAt.description: The date and time when the student submitted this specific answer.

definition.description: Collection storing the Firebase Auth UIDs of users designated as administrators. Documents exist only for admins. Used for database authorization (DBAC - existence over content).

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the admin user.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all master exam definitions. Admins have full CRUD access. Includes fields like `activeStartDate` and `activeEndDate` for exam availability.

params.name: examId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exam.

definition.description: Subcollection containing master question definitions for a specific exam. Admins have full CRUD access. Includes `examId` for explicit linkage. These documents are copied to student attempts.

params.name: examId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent exam.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the question.

definition.description: Subcollection containing master multiple-choice options for a specific question. Admins have full CRUD access. Includes `examId` and `questionId` for explicit linkage. `isCorrect` indicates the correct answer. These documents are copied to student attempts.

params.name: examId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent exam.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent question.

params.name: optionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the multiple-choice option.

definition.description: Document storing the correct answer for an open-ended question. This data is strictly for admin-only access and is never exposed to students. Includes `examId` and `questionId` for explicit linkage.

params.name: examId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent exam.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent question.

definition.description: Document storing the correct final answer and AI evaluation criteria for an explanation-based question. This data is strictly for admin-only access and is never exposed to students. Includes `examId` and `questionId` for explicit linkage.

params.name: examId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent exam.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent question.

definition.description: Subcollection for unique 6-character alphanumeric access codes. Admins create and manage. Students use these codes for one-time exam entry and results viewing. Includes `examId` for explicit linkage. `isUsedForEntry` and `studentAttemptId` are updated by students during exam start.

params.name: examId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent exam.

params.name: accessCodeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exam access code.

definition.description: Top-level collection representing a student's single attempt at an exam. This is the root for all student-owned, in-progress exam data. Includes denormalized `studentAuthUid` (Firebase anonymous UID) for authorization independence, `examId`, and `examAccessCodeId` for context.

params.name: studentAttemptId
params.description: The unique identifier for the student's exam attempt.

definition.description: Subcollection containing copies of exam questions specific to a student's attempt. Includes denormalized `studentAuthUid`, `examId`, and `studentAttemptId` for robust, authorization-independent access control by the student and QAP compliance for lists.

params.name: studentAttemptId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent student attempt.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the question within this attempt.

definition.description: Subcollection containing copies of multiple-choice options for a specific question within a student's attempt. Includes denormalized `studentAuthUid`, `examId`, `studentAttemptId`, and `questionId` for robust, authorization-independent access control.

params.name: studentAttemptId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent student attempt.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent question within this attempt.

params.name: optionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the multiple-choice option.

definition.description: Subcollection recording a student's submitted answers for questions within their attempt. Includes denormalized `studentAuthUid`, `examId`, `studentAttemptId`, and `questionId` for robust, authorization-independent access control by the student and QAP compliance for lists.

params.name: studentAttemptId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent student attempt.

params.name: studentAnswerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the student's answer.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for ImtahanFlow is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and adherence to QAP (Query as Permission) principles. This is achieved through strategic denormalization and a clear separation of administrative and student data paths.

**Authorization Independence and Denormalization Strategy:**

1.  **Admin Role:** A top-level `roles_admin/{adminUid}` collection is used for Database Access Control (DBAC). An admin's Firebase Authentication UID (derived from a pre-registered admin user after a successful login via the secret URL and password) must exist as a document ID in this collection. This enables straightforward rules like `allow read, write: if exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid));`, eliminating complex `get()` calls for admin authorization.

2.  **Exam Definitions (`exams/{examId}` and subcollections):** These collections (`Exam`, `Question`, `MultipleChoiceOption`, `OpenEndedQuestionCorrectAnswer`, `ExplanationQuestionCriteria`) are primarily for administrative CRUD operations. Their authorization relies solely on the `roles_admin` check. Students **cannot** directly access questions or correct answers from these master collections. This segregation ensures that sensitive information (correct answers) is never exposed to students.

3.  **Exam Access Codes (`exams/{examId}/accessCodes/{accessCodeId}`):** These documents include `examId` for context. A student can `get` an `accessCodeId` document by querying its unique `code` field. The student can then *atomically update* the `isUsedForEntry` and `studentAttemptId` fields only if `isUsedForEntry` is currently `false`. This design allows for a secure, one-time usage mechanism without complex `get()` calls to other collections for authorization during the initial entry.

4.  **Student Attempts (`studentAttempts/{studentAttemptId}` and subcollections):** This is the core of student-specific data and a critical area for denormalization to achieve Authorization Independence and QAPs.
    *   **`StudentAttempt` documents (e.g., `studentAttempts/{studentAttemptId}`):** Each `StudentAttempt` document *must* contain the `studentAuthUid` field, which stores the Firebase anonymous authentication UID of the student taking the exam. This `studentAuthUid` is generated and assigned upon the student's first use of an `ExamAccessCode` to start an exam. It also denormalizes `examId` and `examAccessCodeId` for complete context within the document.
    *   **Student-Specific Questions and Options (`studentAttempts/{studentAttemptId}/questions/{questionId}` and `studentAttempts/{studentAttemptId}/questions/{questionId}/multipleChoiceOptions/{optionId}`):** When a student begins an exam, copies of the relevant `Question` and `MultipleChoiceOption` documents (excluding correct answers) are created under their specific `studentAttempts/{studentAttemptId}` path. Crucially, each copied `Question` and `MultipleChoiceOption` document **includes denormalized fields**: `studentAuthUid`, `examId`, `studentAttemptId`, and `questionId` (for options). This extensive denormalization means that rules for these documents can simply check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.studentAuthUid` or `request.auth.uid == get(/databases/$(database)/documents/studentAttempts/$(studentAttemptId)).data.studentAuthUid`. This ensures QAP compliance for listing all questions and options pertinent to *their specific attempt*.
    *   **Student Answers (`studentAttempts/{studentAttemptId}/studentAnswers/{studentAnswerId}`):** Each `StudentAnswer` document similarly **includes denormalized fields**: `studentAuthUid`, `examId`, `studentAttemptId`, and `questionId`. This enables direct rule checks like `request.auth.uid == resource.data.studentAuthUid` for creating, updating, and reading answers, preventing any `get()` calls to other root collections.

**QAPs (Query as Permission) Support:**

*   **Admin Listing:** Admins can list all `Exams`, `Questions`, `AccessCodes`, `StudentAttempts`, and `StudentAnswers` by applying the global admin role check (`exists(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`). This is inherently QAP compliant as the `list` operation is permitted if the user is an admin.
*   **Student Listing:** Students are restricted from broad `list` operations. Instead:
    *   To start an exam: They `get` a specific `ExamAccessCode` by its known `code` (client-side query). This is not a `list` against a collection where security relies on filtering results.
    *   To take an exam/view questions: Once a `StudentAttempt` is established, the student can `list` questions and options from `studentAttempts/{studentAttemptId}/questions`. Because each document in this path (and its subcollections) contains the `studentAuthUid`, the rule `request.auth.uid == resource.data.studentAuthUid` allows the list to proceed, ensuring all returned documents belong to the authenticated student, fulfilling QAP.
    *   To submit/view answers: Similarly, students can `list` their `studentAnswers` from `studentAttempts/{studentAttemptId}/studentAnswers` using the `request.auth.uid == resource.data.studentAuthUid` check, which is QAP compliant.
